news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 2026 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred
news.summary: NeurAxis's board authorized a Q2 2026 stock dividend on its Series B Preferred Stock, to be paid in roughly 80,463 common shares rather than cash, based on a $2.38 conversion rate. The dividend is payable July 29, 2026 to holders of record as of July 21, 2026.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At its June 10, 2026 annual meeting in Carmel, Indiana, NeurAxis shareholders re-elected all six director nominees and approved expanding the 2022 Omnibus Incentive Plan's share pool from about 1.48 million to 3.4 million shares (extending its evergreen feature to 2033), plus a new 2025 Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

news.headline: NeurAxis Reports Record First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results Driven by 80% Revenue Growth
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue hit a record $1.6 million (up 80% YoY, unit deliveries up 35%), gross margin expanded to 86.4%, and net loss narrowed to $1.76 million, powered by the new Category I CPT code for PENFS reimbursement and a VA Federal Supply Schedule ramp management said is running ahead of plan. The accompanying 10-Q still carried a management/auditor going-concern qualification -- despite $7.1 million of cash on hand, auditors want proof of 12 months of self-sustaining operating cash flow before lifting it -- and Craig-Hallum reiterated Buy and a $13 price target afterward, still excluding VA revenue from its model.

news.headline: NeurAxis Expands Payer Coverage with Four New Medical Policies
news.summary: NeurAxis announced four additional insurer medical policies covering PENFS/IB-Stim, adding roughly 1.25 million covered lives across West Virginia, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia and Florida. The move continues the company's push to broaden reimbursement access following the Category I CPT code taking effect January 1, 2026.

news.headline: NeurAxis Increases At-The-Market Offering Capacity to $11.5 Million
news.summary: NeurAxis filed a prospectus supplement raising the ceiling on its Craig-Hallum-run ATM equity program to $11.5 million, after having sold 1,125,281 shares for roughly $5.8-6.3 million since the facility launched in August 2025. The expanded capacity gives the company more flexibility to fund operations as it scales commercially.

news.headline: NeurAxis CEO and CFO Buy Shares in the Open Market
news.summary: CEO Brian Carrico purchased 8,060 shares at $1.96 apiece, bringing his direct stake to 348,178 shares, while CFO Timothy Henrichs made his first open-market purchase of 7,593 shares the same day. The coordinated buying signaled management confidence ahead of the Q1 growth acceleration reported the following month.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings call (estimated)
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis has not yet issued a press release scheduling its Q2 2026 results call as of July 23, 2026. Last year's Q2 2025 call was hosted August 12, 2025, and this year's Q1 2026 call landed May 12, so most trackers project the Q2 2026 report around August 11-12, 2026, with no official confirmation yet.

catalysts.label: Series B preferred stock dividend payment
catalysts.detail: The already-declared Q2 2026 stock dividend (~80,463 common shares) pays out to Series B preferred holders of record as of July 21, 2026.

catalysts.label: Additional payer medical-policy wins
catalysts.detail: Company continues pursuing new insurer coverage decisions for PENFS/IB-Stim to further expand covered lives beyond current levels (roughly 100 million lives covered as of early 2026); no new policy wins have been announced since the April 22 update.

catalysts.label: VA Federal Supply Schedule ramp
catalysts.detail: Continued scale-up of IB-Stim sales through the VA's Federal Supply Schedule contract (effective December 1, 2025), which management said in May was tracking ahead of initial expectations; this revenue stream is not yet reflected in sell-side models (e.g., Craig-Hallum's price target). Independent analysts estimate even low-single-digit penetration of the eligible VA population could add several million dollars of incremental annual revenue by 2027-2028.

catalysts.label: Cash-flow breakeven trajectory / ~$15M revenue run-rate target
catalysts.detail: Management has framed roughly $15 million in annualized revenue as the level needed to reach cash-flow breakeven, alongside guidance for a monthly cash burn of about $1 million or less through the rest of 2026 -- a milestone investors will track via upcoming quarterly reports, and one relevant to resolving the going-concern qualification on the balance sheet.

catalysts.label: Lytham Partners investor conference participation
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis has participated in Lytham Partners' Fall Investor Conference in each of the last two years (most recently September 30, 2025); a similar Fall 2026 appearance is likely but has not yet been formally announced.

pulse: NeurAxis remains in a quiet stretch heading into late July: a fresh re-check on July 23 across SEC filings, press releases, and news aggregators turns up no company-specific developments beyond what was already known -- the June 10 annual-meeting votes (director re-elections, expanded incentive plan) and the routine Q2 Series B preferred stock dividend declared July 9 (paying July 29 to holders of record July 21) remain the only items in the past six weeks, with no new payer wins, VA updates, FDA news, financings, or an official Q2 2026 earnings date. The stock trades around $6.80-$7.30 (52-week range $2.20-$9.33, high set June 27), with the underlying growth story staying intact and reimbursement-driven (the new Category I CPT code, roughly 100 million covered lives, an ahead-of-plan VA Federal Supply Schedule ramp), tempered by the going-concern qualification still attached to the Q1 10-Q despite $7.1 million of cash on hand. Analysts (Craig-Hallum et al.) hold Buy/Strong Buy ratings with price targets of $9-$13 that largely exclude VA revenue, and the next real catalyst is Q2 2026 earnings, only estimated for around August 11-12 based on last year's cadence (not yet officially confirmed), which should show whether CPT-code and VA tailwinds are still accelerating unit growth and whether cash runway has improved enough to address the going-concern language.
